ai_overview: NOTE: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email could not be retrieved — Microsoft 365 authentication is still pending, and this digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only. Tuesday 28 April was an active day for the product and design team. Ciara Carroll was the standout contributor despite officially commencing leave: she posted across four channels (#gdc-pod, #gdc-product-design-pod, #gdc-product-led-growth) sharing CA/FR oplist card designs, requesting a new Jira ticket for a Corey UX issue, and actively participating in the Claude Design AI prompt-logging initiative Mike kicked off. Her Jira ticket GDCU-8801 (Oplist Redesign CA/FR) moved to Done. Chloe Christie was equally active, posting the midweek catch-up agenda in #6amigos, retro links in #gdc-pod, flagging a text colour bug on gambling.com/no, and facilitating the UX weekly sync via Confluence. Josh Smith raised an image-width regression on the social casino page and posted the UX sync doc. On the dev side, Nebojsa Stanic had the most significant Jira movement: two tickets Done (GDCU-8915 GEO selector for Card Carousel Shortcode, GDCU-8836 Card Carousel Shortcode) plus two more active tickets (In Progress and In Product). Jessica Dordevic has two active Jira tickets progressing (GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners In Progress, GDCU-8879 Brand Carousel Component In Product). Novica Vukobratovic was out sick on 27 April (his child also ill), with no visible activity on 28 April. Colin Brannigan was active on 27 April evening, prepping for a Q1 earnings call and requesting wins data from all division leads. 13 of 22 tracked people showed activity across the period.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Remains on medical sick leave (signed off by her doctor on 21 April). No Jira or Slack activity on 28 April. Mike confirmed in #gdc-product-design-pod that Vic is due back on Tuesday 29 April, and flagged that a Corey data analysis task will be handed to her on her return. Victoria Dadson joined #core-product on 17 December 2025; no messages visible since then.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: No visible Slack messages on 28 April, but two active Jira tickets updated within the last 24 hours: GDCU-8928 ([GDC][UX] IUJ Banners — In Progress) and GDCU-8879 ([GDC][UX] Create Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card — In Product). In #gdc-high-agency her last post was 27 March sharing insights on Figma + Claude Code MCP workflows for component prototyping. Her core focus currently spans IUJ personalisation banners and component library work.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Active on 27 April in #gdc-product-led-growth, demonstrating a Claude Design workflow to Mike: he showed how a quiz hub prototype (hub page, quiz pages, final page) can be generated in ~15 minutes and iterated quickly before finalising in Figma, describing the process as '3 hours to 15 minutes.' Mike tagged him on 28 April to log prompts used in claude.ai/design into Confluence. Jira: GDCU-8904 ([GDC][UX] Group Bets Widget) is In Progress and was updated recently. Also has input on NZ header logos and image sizing discussed in #gdc-product-design-pod.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Despite officially starting leave from Monday 28 April, Ciara was highly active all day. In #gdc-pod (11:44) she asked for current site breakpoints for dev-ready Figma files. In #gdc-product-design-pod she requested a new Jira ticket for Corey's UX concerns about the Snapchat campaign landing page (11:19), shared an image asset (11:30), and discussed operator logo sizing/widths for the NZ header (16:46–16:47). In #gdc-product-led-growth she agreed with Mike's suggestion to document Claude Design gaps in an md guidelines site (09:59), said she'd build a page listing unfinished work (11:53), and shared the CA/FR op list card designs from Figma (16:46). Jira: GDCU-8801 (Oplist Redesign CA/FR Casino Page) is Done; GDCU-8432 (Geo Locate Images on Global HP) is In Progress; GDCU-8736 (Imagery for Learning Hub) is In Product; GDCU-8619 (Operator List Redesign) is In Product.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Active on 28 April. In #gdc-product-design-pod (09:32) he posted the UX Weekly Sync Confluence doc for the 28 April meeting, asking the team to add discussion points. Later (16:30) he flagged that an editor reported images not sitting full width on gambling.com/us/online-casinos/social after a recent update, asking whether the fix sits with UX or editors — Ciara confirmed it needs an objects width update. Also in #core-product (21 April) he posted the product sync agenda. Continues to manage DC8 priorities and UX sync cadence.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Highly active on 28 April across multiple channels. In #6amigos (09:12) she posted the midweek catch-up agenda for 29 April. In #gdc-pod (09:13) she posted the retro link. In #gdc-nonen-sync (10:13) she flagged that body text is appearing brown on gambling.com/no/spilleautomater/jawsome-pirates, asking the team to check the content. In #gdc-product-design-pod (09:32) she asked the team to add points to the UX sync doc, (16:30) asked who maintains images for the social casino page, and (17:28) commented that she is more concerned with raw HTML editors put on the page rather than the image sizing issue. Also has extensive DC8 planning activity earlier in the week.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: No visible Slack activity on 28 April. Three Jira tickets updated recently: GDCU-8733 ([GDC][DEV] Apply Feedback to the Learning Hub — In Progress), GDCU-8871 ([GDC][DEV] Free to Play App POC v2 — In Product), and GDCU-8806 ([GDC][DEV] Implement In-App Age Gate — Backlog). His main ongoing work is the FTP App and Learning Hub feedback implementation. No DM or channel messages visible in April so far.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: No visible Slack activity on 28 April. Last visible Slack post was 22 April in #gdc-pod where he identified that broken pages on gambling.com/is were a content issue (editors added raw HTML). No Jira tickets updated in the last 24 hours. He is the channel creator for #gdc-high-agency and oversees the high-agency dev approach. No DM activity visible in recent weeks.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: No visible Slack activity on 28 April. Two Jira tickets updated recently: GDCU-8834 ([GDC][UX][DEV] Implement Fixes for Cool Tones Update — In QA) and GDCU-8842 ([GDC][DEV][ALL GEOS] Integrate Genesis Content Layouts — In Product). The Cool Tones Update fixes are in QA indicating active dev work. Previously he gave a thorough technical explanation of Cosmo Wheel RNG mechanics (23–24 April) in response to Mike's question about guaranteeing a single UK winner.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No visible Slack activity on 28 April. One Jira ticket updated recently: GDCU-8900 ([GDC][DEV] SlotsLaunch Game Grid Shortcode — To Do). Gabriel mentioned in #gdc-pod on 24 April that 'Kelvin knows' regarding Bloomreach access, suggesting he was around. In #gdc-nonen-sync (26 March) he flagged a blocked sprint release due to a deleted author on two Greek article records. No DMs visible since 9 April.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. Gabriel noted in #gdc-pdmt on 13 April that Milan is on annual leave, explaining potential spare capacity in the sprint. His last visible Slack activity was 13 March in #gdc-high-agency when he flagged that 5pm meetings are too late for Serbia. No Jira assignments or channel posts found for the past month.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: No visible Slack activity on 28 April, but significant Jira movement: GDCU-8915 ([GDC][DEV] Add GEO selector to Card Carousel Shortcode — Done) and GDCU-8836 ([GDC][DEV] Create a Card Carousel Shortcode — Done) both completed. Additionally, GDCU-8870 ([GDC][DEV] Investigate Performance Improvements for Page Time — In Product) and GDCU-8924 ([GDC][DEV] Implement feedback for Origins API integration — In Progress) are active. Two deliveries in a single Jira window is strong output.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: No activity on 28 April. On 27 April he posted in #gdc-pod that he is sick along with his child and is taking a day off. On 22–23 April he was active in #gdc-pod: on 22 April he identified that broken Iceland page content was a 'def content' issue and made a joke about 'MS Word alignment issues'; on 23 April Shane confirmed QA was done post-release and noted Novica was working on mobile bottom nav bumps. No Jira tickets visible in the last-24h results.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. Most recent channel activity was 8 April in #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project where she reported the /api/mcp/deep_links endpoint for Bookies brand returning responses consistently above 5 seconds — a performance concern flagged to Alex Kornilov. No Jira tickets in the recent update window. No DMs or channel posts visible since early April.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Low activity on 28 April. In #gdc-pod (09:01) she asked if anyone knows who receives authentication codes sent to gdccoredev@gdcgroup.com (for ChatGPT) — no response visible. Jira: GDCU-8866 (Automation bug fixing and refactoring Sprint 8 — In Product) is active. In #gdc-high-agency she joined on 6 March. No DM activity with Mike visible.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: No visible activity on 28 April. Last visible Slack posts: on 24 April in #gdc-pod he asked how to get Bloomreach access (Gabriel replied 'Kelvin knows'). On 23 April he confirmed QA had finished verifying all tickets post-release and gave the site a general sanity check, noting one or two mobile bottom nav bumps that Novica was addressing. In #prism-cms-feedback (30–31 March) he filed multiple detailed QA bug reports. Active and thorough QA contributor.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. Most recent engagement was in #gdc-f2p-apps on 15 April where he suggested restructuring the F2P app as a Cosmo-branded slots app with a slots library and free spins/slot offers. Gabriel acknowledged Dean's feedback positively. Previously (24 April DM with Mike) he asked about the Stable Stars dedicated page timeline and raised that 3 of the current Cosmo Wheel iPhone winners are internal.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. In #gdc-learning-hub (19 March) he said he wouldn't finish his learning hub review by that week and set a new deadline of 3 April, noting much of the AI-generated content was misleading, unintuitive, or over-explained. Most recent DM with Mike was 24 April: flagged that the Bet365 T&C scrollable fix on qa10 still doesn't resolve the grid layout case, and was asked by Mike to complete a stakeholder review form for the Tinder Widget design (GDCU-8546).

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. In #gdc-f2p-apps (15 April) he reported that some F2P games don't work and that he had no Cosmo Spins (24-hour wait bug after login). Last DM with Mike was March 2026. Colin asked him about Accuranker access in #core-division-leads on 15 April. No Jira or Slack activity visible for 28 April.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: No visible activity on 28 April. Last visible Slack was 24 April in #gdc-pod (replied 'Kelvin knows' re Bloomreach access). In #gdc-pdmt (20 April) he repurposed the PDMT meeting for planning sessions and team weeklies, noting there are already enough alignment meetings. In #gdc-f2p-apps (15 April) he provided positive feedback on Dean's Cosmo app restructure idea and confirmed the app would be smoke-tested before launch. In #prism-cms-feedback he confirmed all reported issues are fixed and explained the Change Set feature. Active manager and lead contributor to Prism CMS.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: No observable activity on 28 April. Most recent channel activity was 16 April in #core-division where she announced the Iceland geo launch on gambling.com/is, crediting the product team, commercial team, and Genesis team alongside the non-EN team. She created the #gdc-nonen-market-updates channel on 21 April for daily industry updates across non-EN markets. The channel itself is automated and active. No DMs or posts visible since 22 April.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Active on 27 April evening. In #core-division-leads (21:28) he posted a detailed request for Q1 earnings call material, asking all division leads to contribute bullet points across AI Transformation, Channel Diversification, GEO Growth, SEO, and Product & Campaigns. He also shared a March P&L revenue review link (11:38) and nudged teams to share wins in #core-division (15:49). On 24 April he shared an X/Twitter video, commented on Dom's betting bot win, and flagged strong concerns about NZ affiliate marketing regulation potentially closing the entire licensed channel. Most recent DM with Mike was 24 April discussing Claude Design concepts.
